description:
This pie chart illustrates the distribution of total observed land cover change processes across Europe for the combined periods 2006-2012 and 2012-2018. The chart displays 18 distinct change categories, each with its percentage share of the total changes.

The largest shares are:
*   Agricultural intensification: 16%
*   Tree encroachment: 16%
*   Agricultural conversion to cropland: 14%
*   Urbanization: 13%
*   Expansion of grassland: 9%
*   Intensification of grassland use: 7%
*   De-urbanization: 4%
*   Agricultural conversion to managed grassland: 4%
*   Forest increase - Natural succession: 3%
*   Woody plant encroachment: 3%
*   Scrub encroachment: 2%
*   Grassland degradation: 2%
*   Extensification of grassland use: 2%

Smaller categories, each representing 1% of the total change, include:
*   Agricultural extensification
*   Occupation by water infrastructures / Hydrological restructuring
*   Drainage
*   Hydrological restoration
*   Other

The chart highlights that agricultural changes (intensification, conversion to cropland, extensification, conversion to managed grassland) account for a significant portion of the total land cover dynamics. Tree and woody plant encroachment, along with forest increase, also represent substantial shares. Urbanization and de-urbanization processes demonstrate dynamic land use changes.
